questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.topic: Quantitative Aptitude — Time, Speed and Distance
questions.stem: Two trains $60$ km apart move toward each other at $40$ km/h and $20$ km/h. The time taken to meet is _____ hours.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Closing speed = sum of speeds when moving toward each other.

**Working.** Relative speed $=40+20=60$ km/h; time $=\dfrac{60}{60}=1$ h.

**Answer.** $1$ hour.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: The arrow shown points upward (north). After rotating it $180^\circ$, it points

figure.caption: A vertical arrow pointing upward.
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 80 160' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' role='img'><line x1='40' y1='130' x2='40' y2='30' stroke='#10b981' stroke-width='3'/><polygon points='40,30 32,45 48,45' fill='#10b981'/></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** A $180^\circ$ rotation reverses the direction.

**Working.** Upward becomes downward.

**Answer.** Downward (south).

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: SEISMOGRAPH : EARTHQUAKE :: ANEMOMETER : ?
questions.solution:
**Concept.** The relationship is 'instrument : phenomenon it records/measures'.

**Working.** A seismograph records earthquakes; an anemometer measures wind speed.

**Answer.** wind speed.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: All cats are animals. Some animals are wild. Which conclusion is valid?
options: No definite conclusion about cats being wild can be drawn
questions.solution:
**Concept.** A particular premise about the broader set need not apply to the subset.

**Working.** 'Some animals are wild' does not specify whether any are cats, so nothing definite about wild cats follows.

**Answer.** No definite conclusion can be drawn.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem:
Read the passage and answer.

'Critics argue that subsidies for fossil-fuel extraction distort energy markets and delay the transition to renewables. Proponents counter that such subsidies protect jobs and keep energy affordable in the short term.'

The passage primarily presents:
options: two opposing viewpoints on fossil-fuel subsidies
a definitive argument against renewable energy
a neutral historical account of energy policy
evidence proving subsidies have no effect
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Identify the rhetorical structure (argument, narrative, comparison of views).

**Working.** The passage states what 'critics argue' and what 'proponents counter', i.e., two opposing positions, without endorsing either.

**Answer.** two opposing viewpoints on fossil-fuel subsidies.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: The angle between the hour and minute hands of a clock at exactly $3{:}00$ is _____ degrees.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Each hour mark spans $30^\circ$.

**Working.** At $3{:}00$ the hands are $3$ hour-marks apart: $3\times 30^\circ = 90^\circ$.

**Answer.** $90$.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: A is twice as efficient as B, and together they finish a job in $12$ days. A alone would take
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Let B's rate be $x$; then A's rate is $2x$.

**Working.** $3x=\dfrac{1}{12}\Rightarrow x=\dfrac{1}{36}$; A's rate $=2x=\dfrac{1}{18}$, so A alone takes $18$ days.

**Answer.** $18$ days.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.topic: Quantitative Aptitude — CI vs SI Difference
questions.stem: The difference between the compound interest and the simple interest on $10000$ at $10\%$ per annum for $2$ years is _____ .
questions.solution:
**Concept.** For $2$ years the difference is $P\left(\tfrac{r}{100}\right)^2$.

**Working.**
$$10000\times (0.1)^2 = 10000\times 0.01 = 100$$

**Answer.** $100$.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: ORE : SMELTING :: WHEAT : ?
questions.solution:
**Concept.** The relationship is 'raw material : the processing step that yields a usable product'.

**Working.** Smelting processes ore into metal; milling processes wheat into flour. Harvesting/sowing are field operations, not refining.

**Answer.** milling.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: Choose the correct word: 'The blast had a serious ____ on the surrounding structures.'
questions.solution:
**Concept.** 'Effect' is the noun (a result); 'affect' is usually the verb (to influence).

**Working.** A noun is needed after 'a serious', so 'effect' is correct.

**Answer.** effect.

questions.subject: Mine Surveying & Geomatics
questions.stem: For a modern internal-focusing tacheometer, the stadia constants are usually
options: both equal to the focal length
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Constants are set by the stadia hair spacing and optics.

**Reasoning.** The multiplying constant $K=f/i\approx100$; internal focusing makes the additive constant $C\approx0$, simplifying $D=Ks$.

**Answer.** K ≈ 100, C ≈ 0.

questions.subject: Mine Ventilation
questions.stem: A rectangular airway is $4\,\text{m}$ wide and $3\,\text{m}$ high. Its perimeter is _____ m.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Perimeter $=2(w+h)$.

**Working.**
$$2(4+3)=14.$$

**Answer.** $14\,\text{m}$.

questions.subject: Mine Ventilation
questions.stem: A fan develops $500\ \text{Pa}$ at its base speed; the speed is increased by a factor of $1.2$ (figure). Compute the new pressure (Pa).

figure.caption: Fan pressure characteristic
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 200 130' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><line x1='30' y1='100' x2='180' y2='100' stroke='#475569'/><line x1='30' y1='30' x2='30' y2='100' stroke='#475569'/><path d='M30 95 Q100 60 175 40' fill='none' stroke='#2563eb' stroke-width='2'/><text x='34' y='120' font-size='8'>p1=500, speed x1.2</text></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** $p\propto N^2$.

**Working.**
$$p_2=500\times1.2^2=500\times1.44=720\ \text{Pa}$$

**Answer.** $720$ Pa.

questions.subject: Drilling & Blasting
questions.stem: Which measures help reduce blast-induced ground vibration at nearby structures? (Select all that apply.)
options: Reducing the maximum charge fired per delay
Using delay detonators instead of instantaneous firing
Firing all holes simultaneously on a single delay
Increasing the distance between the blast and the structure
questions.solution:
**Concept.** PPV falls with smaller charge per delay and larger scaled distance.

**Reasoning.** Limiting the charge per delay and sequencing with delays both lower the instantaneous charge, while greater distance increases the scaled distance — all reduce PPV. Firing everything on one delay maximises the instantaneous charge and worsens vibration.

**Answer.** Options 1, 2 and 4.

questions.subject: Mine Environment & Occupational Health
questions.stem: A flat seam lies at depth $H=200\ \text{m}$ and the angle of draw is $25^\circ$ (figure). Compute the half-width of the surface influence zone measured from the panel edge (m, two decimals).

figure.caption: Angle of draw
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 220 150' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><rect x='20' y='30' width='180' height='12' fill='#cbd5e1'/><rect x='90' y='120' width='40' height='10' fill='#1f2937'/><g stroke='#dc2626' stroke-dasharray='3 2'><line x1='110' y1='125' x2='60' y2='36'/><line x1='110' y1='125' x2='160' y2='36'/></g><text x='32' y='145' font-size='8'>H=200, draw angle 25 deg</text></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** The influence half-width from a point is $H\tan(\text{angle of draw})$.

**Working.**
$$x=200\tan25^\circ=200\times0.4663=93.26\ \text{m}$$

**Answer.** $93.26$ m.

questions.subject: Underground Mining
questions.stem: Using the Bieniawski formula Sp = S1(0.64 + 0.36 w/h) with S1 = 5 MPa and w/h = 5, the pillar strength, in MPa, is ____ (one decimal).
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Bieniawski pillar strength: $S_p=S_1(0.64+0.36\,w/h)$.

**Working.**
$$S_p=5\,(0.64+0.36\times5)=5\times2.44=12.2\ \text{MPa}$$

**Answer.** 12.2 MPa.

questions.subject: Drilling & Blasting
questions.stem: Deck charging (decking) of a blasthole means
options: filling the whole hole with one continuous charge
splitting the charge into separate decks separated by inert stemming, often fired on different delays
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Decking distributes explosive along the hole.

**Reasoning.** Inert stemming separates the column into decks (sometimes on different delays); this distributes energy, can avoid weak/waste bands, and reduces the charge weight per delay for vibration control.

**Answer.** Charge split into decks by stemming.

questions.subject: Mine Surveying & Geomatics
questions.stem: For a perfectly balanced closed-loop traverse, the algebraic sums of latitudes and of departures should each equal
questions.solution:
**Concept.** A loop returns to its origin.

**Reasoning.** Since the traverse closes on its starting point, the net north–south travel and the net east–west travel are both zero; non-zero sums are the misclosure to be adjusted.

**Answer.** Zero.

questions.subject: Mine Economics, Planning & Systems Engineering
questions.stem: Equipment has a constant failure rate $\lambda=0.002$ per hour. Its mean time between failures (MTBF) is _____ hours.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** $\text{MTBF}=\dfrac{1}{\lambda}$.

**Working.**
$$\frac{1}{0.002}=500.$$

**Answer.** $500$ hours.

questions.subject: Underground Mining
questions.stem: For the longwall panel shown, the face length is 200 m, the shearer web (depth of cut) is 0.8 m, the seam is 2 m thick and the in-situ density is $1.5\ \mathrm{t/m^3}$. The coal produced in one full web pass, in tonnes, is _____.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 280 180' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><rect x='30' y='30' width='220' height='120' fill='#fef3c7' stroke='#b45309'/><line x1='30' y1='30' x2='30' y2='150' stroke='#1f2937' stroke-width='6'/><line x1='250' y1='30' x2='250' y2='150' stroke='#1f2937' stroke-width='6'/><line x1='30' y1='90' x2='250' y2='90' stroke='#dc2626' stroke-width='3'/><text x='140' y='84' font-size='11' text-anchor='middle' fill='#dc2626'>face (length 200 m)</text><text x='8' y='95' font-size='9'>gate</text><text x='254' y='95' font-size='9'>gate</text><text x='140' y='135' font-size='10' text-anchor='middle'>panel</text></svg>
figure.caption: Longwall panel (plan)

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Production $=$ face length $\times$ web $\times$ seam thickness $\times$ density.

**Working.**
$$T=200\times0.8\times2\times1.5=480\ \text{t}$$

**Answer.** $480\ \text{t}$.

questions.subject: Mining Geology
questions.stem: Cleavage of a mineral is the
options: tendency to break along definite planes of structural weakness, producing smooth flat surfaces
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Cleavage reflects internal bonding.

**Reasoning.** Where bonds are weakest, the mineral splits along parallel planes; e.g. mica shows perfect basal cleavage, galena cubic cleavage. It is diagnostic in identification.

**Answer.** Breaking along planes of weakness.

questions.subject: Surface Mining
questions.stem: Each dumper carries a payload of 50 t and makes 5 trips per hour. Its hourly haulage, in t/h, is ____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Output $=$ payload $\times$ trips/h.

**Working.**
$$Q=50\times5=250\ \text{t/h}$$

**Answer.** 250 t/h.

questions.subject: Underground Mining
questions.stem: For the bord-and-pillar layout shown with square pillars 20 m wide separated by 5 m wide galleries, compute the extraction ratio.

figure.caption: Bord-and-pillar plan
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 200 200' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><rect x='10' y='10' width='180' height='180' fill='#e5e7eb'/><g fill='#1f2937'><rect x='30' y='30' width='50' height='50'/><rect x='110' y='30' width='50' height='50'/><rect x='30' y='110' width='50' height='50'/><rect x='110' y='110' width='50' height='50'/></g><text x='86' y='60' font-size='9' fill='#475569'>B=5</text><text x='42' y='58' font-size='9' fill='#fff'>W=20</text></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** For square pillars, extraction ratio $e = 1 - \left(\dfrac{W}{W+B}\right)^2$.

**Working.**
$$e = 1 - \left(\frac{20}{25}\right)^2 = 1 - 0.64 = 0.36$$

**Answer.** $\mathbf{0.36}$ (36%).

questions.subject: Mine Environment & Occupational Health
questions.stem: The most effective primary measure to control airborne dust generation at a drilling/cutting point is
options: wet drilling / water sprays that suppress the dust at the point of generation
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Suppress dust at source.

**Reasoning.** Adding water at the cutting/drilling point wets the fines so they do not become airborne; combined with ventilation and respirators this is the front-line control. Dry drilling does the opposite.

**Answer.** Wet drilling / water sprays.

questions.subject: Mine Ventilation
questions.stem: An airway of resistance $0.3\,\text{Ns}^2/\text{m}^8$ carries $15\,\text{m}^3$/s. The pressure loss is _____ Pa.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** $P=RQ^2$.

**Working.**
$$P=0.3\times15^2=0.3\times225=67.5.$$

**Answer.** $67.5\,\text{Pa}$.

questions.subject: Drilling & Blasting
questions.stem: Flyrock (rock thrown beyond the intended blast area) is most often caused by
options: too large a burden and too much stemming
insufficient burden/stemming or geological weakness allowing gas to vent and eject rock
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Flyrock results from uncontrolled gas escape.

**Reasoning.** When the burden or stemming is too small, or a weak seam/cavity exists, high-pressure gases blow out and hurl fragments far beyond the muckpile — the leading cause of blasting injuries.

**Answer.** Insufficient burden/stemming or geological weakness.

questions.subject: Rock Mechanics
questions.stem: An infinite dry rock slope of $25^\circ$ has a discontinuity friction angle of $30^\circ$ (figure). Compute the factor of safety against planar sliding (two decimals).

figure.caption: Planar slope
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 220 140' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><polygon points='30,120 180,120 180,40' fill='#d6d3d1' stroke='#475569'/><line x1='30' y1='120' x2='180' y2='40' stroke='#dc2626' stroke-width='2'/><text x='58' y='112' font-size='8'>slope 25 deg</text><text x='90' y='135' font-size='8'>phi=30 deg</text></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** For a dry cohesionless plane $FoS=\dfrac{\tan\phi}{\tan\beta}$.

**Working.**
$$FoS=\frac{\tan30^\circ}{\tan25^\circ}=\frac{0.5774}{0.4663}=1.24$$

**Answer.** $1.24$.

questions.subject: Mine Environment & Occupational Health
questions.stem: For a subsidence factor of 0.6 and an extracted seam thickness of 3 m, the maximum possible surface subsidence, in m, is ____ (one decimal).
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Max subsidence $=$ subsidence factor $\times$ seam thickness.

**Working.**
$$S_{max}=0.6\times3=1.8\ \text{m}$$

**Answer.** 1.8 m.

questions.subject: Mine Ventilation
questions.stem: For a given fan running at variable speed $N$, the quantity delivered varies as
questions.solution:
**Concept.** First fan law.

**Working.** Quantity is directly proportional to speed.

**Answer.** $Q\propto N$.

questions.subject: Surface Mining
questions.stem: With 4 dipper passes at 30 s per pass, the truck loading time, in seconds, is ____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Loading time $=$ passes $\times$ cycle time.

**Working.**
$$t_L=4\times30=120\ \text{s}$$

**Answer.** 120 s (2 min).

questions.subject: Mine Surveying & Geomatics
questions.stem: A theodolite main scale is graduated to 20′ and its vernier has 60 divisions matching 59 main-scale divisions. The least count, in seconds, is ____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Least count $=\dfrac{\text{smallest main division}}{\text{number of vernier divisions}}$.

**Working.**
$$LC=\frac{20'}{60}=\frac{20\times60''}{60}=20''$$

**Answer.** 20 seconds.

questions.subject: Drilling & Blasting
questions.stem: A face round needs 60 holes drilled in 30 min. If each jumbo boom drills one hole every 2 min, the number of booms required is ____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Holes per boom $=\dfrac{\text{time}}{\text{time per hole}}$; booms $=\dfrac{\text{total holes}}{\text{holes per boom}}$.

**Working.**
$$\text{holes/boom}=\frac{30}{2}=15,\quad N=\frac{60}{15}=4$$

**Answer.** 4 booms.

questions.subject: Mine Surveying & Geomatics
questions.stem: Using the 30 m arc definition, a curve of degree 4° has a radius, in m, of ____ (two decimals).

questions.subject: Drilling & Blasting
questions.stem: Using a presplit hole spacing of 10 × hole diameter, the spacing for 100 mm presplit holes, in m, is ____ (one decimal).
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Presplit spacing is much smaller than production spacing.

**Working.**
$$S=10\times0.10=1.0\ \text{m}$$

**Answer.** 1.0 m.

questions.subject: Rock Mechanics
questions.stem: For $\sigma_x=60$, $\sigma_y=20$, $\tau_{xy}=20\,\text{MPa}$, the maximum in-plane shear stress is _____ MPa. Round to two decimals.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** $\tau_{max}=R=\sqrt{\left(\dfrac{\sigma_x-\sigma_y}{2}\right)^2+\tau_{xy}^2}$.

**Working.**
$$\tau_{max}=\sqrt{20^2+20^2}=28.28.$$

**Answer.** $\approx 28.28\,\text{MPa}$.

questions.subject: Underground Mining
questions.stem: A single-rope winder lifts a cage of mass 10 t carrying a 5 t load (as shown). Taking g = 9.81 m/s², compute the static rope tension (kN), ignoring rope weight.

figure.caption: Winding headgear
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 200 200' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><circle cx='100' cy='40' r='18' fill='none' stroke='#475569' stroke-width='3'/><line x1='100' y1='58' x2='100' y2='150' stroke='#475569' stroke-width='2'/><rect x='85' y='150' width='30' height='30' fill='#94a3b8' stroke='#475569'/><text x='122' y='166' font-size='8'>cage 10 t</text><text x='122' y='180' font-size='8'>load 5 t</text></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Static tension = total suspended mass × g.

**Working.**
$$T = (10000 + 5000)(9.81) = 15000 \times 9.81 = 147150\ \text{N} = 147.15\ \text{kN}$$

**Answer.** $\mathbf{147.15}$ kN.

questions.subject: Mine Surveying & Geomatics
questions.stem: A transition (easement) curve is introduced between a straight and a circular curve mainly to
options: gradually change the curvature and introduce superelevation, avoiding sudden lateral acceleration
remove the need for a theodolite
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Curvature must not jump from zero to 1/R.

**Reasoning.** The transition curve gives a radius decreasing gradually from infinity to R, so centrifugal force and superelevation build up smoothly, improving comfort and safety on haul roads/rail.

**Answer.** Gradual curvature and superelevation.

questions.subject: Mine Environment & Occupational Health
questions.stem: Carbon monoxide (whitedamp) is extremely dangerous chiefly because it
options: smells strongly and warns the miner
combines with haemoglobin (forming carboxyhaemoglobin) with an affinity far greater than oxygen, causing asphyxiation
is heavier than air and settles harmlessly
questions.solution:
**Concept.** CO blocks oxygen transport in blood.

**Reasoning.** CO binds haemoglobin about 200–300 times more readily than oxygen, forming carboxyhaemoglobin and starving tissues of oxygen; it is colourless and odourless, giving no warning.

**Answer.** Forms carboxyhaemoglobin (high affinity).

questions.subject: Engineering Mathematics
questions.stem: For $\mathbf{a}=(3,4)$ and $\mathbf{b}=(1,2)$, compute the magnitude of the cross product $|\mathbf{a}\times\mathbf{b}|$ (the parallelogram area).

figure.caption: Parallelogram spanned by two vectors
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 200 150' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><line x1='30' y1='120' x2='180' y2='120' stroke='#475569'/><line x1='30' y1='120' x2='30' y2='20' stroke='#475569'/><polygon points='30,120 120,90 150,30 60,60' fill='#fde68a' fill-opacity='0.5'/><line x1='30' y1='120' x2='120' y2='90' stroke='#2563eb' stroke-width='2' marker-end='url(#cr)'/><line x1='30' y1='120' x2='60' y2='60' stroke='#16a34a' stroke-width='2' marker-end='url(#cr)'/><defs><marker id='cr' markerWidth='8' markerHeight='8' refX='6' refY='3' orient='auto'><path d='M0 0 L6 3 L0 6 Z' fill='#475569'/></marker></defs><text x='40' y='140' font-size='8'>parallelogram area</text></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** In 2D, $|\mathbf{a}\times\mathbf{b}|=|a_xb_y-a_yb_x|$.

**Working.**
$$|\mathbf{a}\times\mathbf{b}|=|3(2)-4(1)|=|6-4|=2$$

**Answer.** $2$.

questions.subject: Drilling & Blasting
questions.stem: A blasthole is charged over an 8 m explosive column with a linear loading density of 5 kg/m (as shown). Compute the charge per hole (kg).

figure.caption: Charged blasthole
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 150 200' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><rect x='55' y='20' width='30' height='160' fill='#e5e7eb' stroke='#475569'/><rect x='55' y='40' width='30' height='40' fill='#cbd5e1'/><text x='90' y='62' font-size='8'>stemming</text><rect x='55' y='80' width='30' height='100' fill='#f59e0b'/><text x='90' y='128' font-size='8'>charge 8 m</text><text x='90' y='143' font-size='8'>5 kg/m</text></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Charge = column length × linear loading density.

**Working.**
$$Q = 8 \times 5 = 40$$

**Answer.** $\mathbf{40}$ kg per hole.

questions.subject: Engineering Mathematics
questions.stem: Given $P(A)=0.3$ and $P(B\,|\,A)=0.5$, the joint probability $P(A\cap B)$ is _____ (round to 2 decimals).
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Multiplication rule $P(A\cap B)=P(A)\,P(B\,|\,A)$.

**Working.**
$$P(A\cap B)=0.3\times 0.5=0.15$$

**Answer.** $0.15$.

questions.subject: Mine Economics & Planning
questions.stem: Two components with reliabilities 0.9 and 0.8 are connected in series as shown. Compute the system reliability.

figure.caption: Series reliability block diagram
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 240 100' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><line x1='10' y1='50' x2='40' y2='50' stroke='#475569' stroke-width='2'/><rect x='40' y='35' width='60' height='30' fill='#e0f2fe' stroke='#475569'/><text x='70' y='54' font-size='9' text-anchor='middle'>R1=0.9</text><line x1='100' y1='50' x2='140' y2='50' stroke='#475569' stroke-width='2'/><rect x='140' y='35' width='60' height='30' fill='#e0f2fe' stroke='#475569'/><text x='170' y='54' font-size='9' text-anchor='middle'>R2=0.8</text><line x1='200' y1='50' x2='230' y2='50' stroke='#475569' stroke-width='2'/></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Series reliability is the product of component reliabilities.

**Working.**
$$R_s = R_1 R_2 = 0.9 \times 0.8 = 0.72$$

**Answer.** $R_s = \mathbf{0.72}$.

questions.subject: Drilling & Blasting
questions.stem: A blasthole of 150 mm diameter is drilled to 12 m depth. The borehole volume, in m³, is ____ (three decimals).
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Volume $=\dfrac{\pi}{4}d^2 L$.

**Working.**
$$V=\frac{\pi}{4}(0.15)^2(12)=0.7854\times0.0225\times12=0.212\ \text{m}^3$$

**Answer.** 0.212 m³.

questions.subject: Drilling & Blasting
questions.stem: A 6 m explosive column has a VOD of 4000 m/s. The time for the detonation to traverse the full column, in milliseconds, is ____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** $t=\dfrac{L}{\text{VOD}}$.

**Working.**
$$t=\frac{6}{4000}=1.5\times10^{-3}\ \text{s}=1.5\ \text{ms}$$

**Answer.** 1.5 ms.

questions.subject: Drilling & Blasting
questions.stem: In a burn cut, the uncharged large-diameter relief hole serves to
options: provide a void/free face into which the charged cut holes can break, improving pull
questions.solution:
**Concept.** The relief hole supplies the only free face for the cut.

**Reasoning.** With no free face except the heading itself, the empty relief hole gives the adjacent charged holes somewhere to throw rock; without it the parallel cut would 'freeze' and the round would fail to pull.

**Answer.** Free face/void for the cut holes to break into.

questions.subject: Rock Mechanics
questions.stem: For a dry cohesionless plane with $\phi=40^\circ$ sliding on a plane dipping $\psi_p=35^\circ$, the factor of safety $\tan\phi/\tan\psi_p$ is _____ . Round to two decimals.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** $\text{FoS}=\dfrac{\tan\phi}{\tan\psi_p}$.

**Working.**
$$\text{FoS}=\frac{\tan 40^\circ}{\tan 35^\circ}=\frac{0.839}{0.700}=1.20.$$

**Answer.** $\approx 1.20$.

questions.subject: Mine Economics & Planning
questions.stem: In the activity-on-node network below (durations in weeks), determine the project duration (length of the critical path) in weeks.

figure.caption: Project network
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 300 160' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><circle cx='30' cy='80' r='14' fill='#e0f2fe' stroke='#475569'/><text x='30' y='84' font-size='9' text-anchor='middle'>S</text><circle cx='130' cy='40' r='16' fill='#fef3c7' stroke='#b45309'/><text x='130' y='44' font-size='8' text-anchor='middle'>A=2</text><circle cx='130' cy='120' r='16' fill='#fef3c7' stroke='#b45309'/><text x='130' y='124' font-size='8' text-anchor='middle'>B=4</text><circle cx='230' cy='40' r='16' fill='#fef3c7' stroke='#b45309'/><text x='230' y='44' font-size='8' text-anchor='middle'>C=3</text><circle cx='230' cy='120' r='16' fill='#fef3c7' stroke='#b45309'/><text x='230' y='124' font-size='8' text-anchor='middle'>D=5</text><circle cx='285' cy='80' r='12' fill='#e0f2fe' stroke='#475569'/><text x='285' y='84' font-size='8' text-anchor='middle'>E</text><g stroke='#475569' fill='none'><path d='M44 74 L114 46'/><path d='M44 86 L114 114'/><path d='M146 40 L214 40'/><path d='M146 120 L214 120'/><path d='M246 46 L275 74'/><path d='M246 114 L275 86'/></g></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** The project duration equals the length of the longest (critical) path through the network.

**Working.**
$$S\text{–}A\text{–}C\text{–}E = 2+3 = 5,\quad S\text{–}B\text{–}D\text{–}E = 4+5 = 9$$

**Answer.** Critical path S–B–D–E gives a project duration of **9 weeks**.

questions.subject: Drilling & Blasting
questions.stem: A 150 mm hole is charged with explosive of density 850 kg/m³ over a charge length of 8 m (fully coupled). The charge mass per hole, in kg, is ____ (one decimal).
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Charge mass $=\dfrac{\pi}{4}d^2 L_c\,\rho$.

**Working.**
$$m=\frac{\pi}{4}(0.15)^2(8)(850)=0.01767\times8\times850=120.2\ \text{kg}$$

**Answer.** 120.2 kg.

questions.subject: Mining Geology
questions.stem: A coal block covers a plan area of $100{,}000\ \mathrm{m^2}$ with seam thickness $2\ \text{m}$ and in-situ density $1.4\ \mathrm{t/m^3}$. Compute the in-situ (geological) reserve in tonnes.

figure.caption: Coal block volume to tonnage
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 220 150' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><polygon points='40,110 150,110 190,80 80,80' fill='#94a3b8' stroke='#475569'/><polygon points='40,110 40,125 150,125 150,110' fill='#cbd5e1' stroke='#475569'/><polygon points='150,110 150,125 190,95 190,80' fill='#9ca3af' stroke='#475569'/><text x='30' y='145' font-size='8'>area=1e5 m^2, t=2 m, density=1.4</text></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Tonnage $=$ area $\times$ thickness $\times$ density.

**Working.**
$$T=100000\times2\times1.4=280000\ \text{t}$$

**Answer.** $280{,}000$ t.

questions.subject: Engineering Mathematics
questions.stem: The alternating series $\displaystyle\sum_{n=1}^{\infty}\frac{(-1)^{n+1}}{n}$ is
questions.solution:
**Concept.** It converges by the alternating-series (Leibniz) test, but $\sum 1/n$ diverges.

**Working.** Terms decrease to $0$ in magnitude, so the alternating series converges (to $\ln 2$); the absolute series is the harmonic series, which diverges. Hence convergence is conditional.

**Answer.** Conditionally convergent.

questions.subject: Rock Mechanics
questions.stem: A computed factor of safety of exactly $1.0$ for a slope indicates
questions.solution:
**Concept.** FoS is resisting over driving forces.

**Working.** $\text{FoS}=1$ means they are equal — the verge of failure.

**Answer.** limiting equilibrium.

questions.subject: Surface Mining
questions.stem: A working bench must accommodate a shovel of working width 10 m, a dumper of width 6 m, and a safety clearance of 4 m. The minimum bench width, in m, is ____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Minimum operating bench width is the sum of equipment footprints plus clearance.

**Working.**
$$W=10+6+4=20\ \text{m}$$

**Answer.** 20 m.

questions.subject: Surface Mining
questions.stem: A target of 750 t/h is required. With each dumper hauling 250 t/h, the number of dumpers needed is ____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Fleet $=\dfrac{\text{required}}{\text{per truck}}$.

**Working.**
$$N=\frac{750}{250}=3$$

**Answer.** 3 dumpers.

questions.subject: Mine Ventilation
questions.stem: Methane is emitted at $0.05\ \mathrm{m^3/s}$ into a roadway. To hold the general-body concentration at or below $1.25\%$ by volume, compute the minimum airflow required ($\mathrm{m^3/s}$).

figure.caption: Dilution of methane
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 240 120' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><rect x='20' y='35' width='200' height='50' fill='#e5e7eb' stroke='#475569'/><line x1='6' y1='60' x2='38' y2='60' stroke='#2563eb' stroke-width='2'/><circle cx='130' cy='75' r='4' fill='#b45309'/><text x='90' y='52' font-size='8'>CH4 0.05 m^3/s</text><text x='30' y='105' font-size='8'>dilute to 1.25%</text></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** $Q\approx\dfrac{\text{gas emission}}{\text{allowable fraction}}$.

**Working.**
$$Q=\frac{0.05}{0.0125}=4\ \mathrm{m^3/s}$$

**Answer.** $4\ \mathrm{m^3/s}$.

questions.subject: Rock Mechanics
questions.stem: For the dry cohesionless infinite slope shown, the slope angle is $\beta=20^\circ$ and the friction angle is $\phi=30^\circ$. The factor of safety ($\tan\phi/\tan\beta$) is _____ (two decimals).

figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 240 150' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><path d='M20 130 L210 60' stroke='#b45309' stroke-width='3' fill='none'/><line x1='20' y1='130' x2='210' y2='130' stroke='#475569'/><path d='M170 130 A50 50 0 0 0 162 110' fill='none' stroke='#dc2626'/><text x='150' y='124' font-size='10' fill='#dc2626'>beta=20°</text><text x='56' y='95' font-size='9'>phi = 30°</text></svg>
figure.caption: Dry cohesionless slope

questions.solution:
**Concept.** For a dry cohesionless infinite slope, $FoS=\dfrac{\tan\phi}{\tan\beta}$.

**Working.**
$$FoS=\frac{\tan 30^\circ}{\tan 20^\circ}=\frac{0.5774}{0.3640}=1.59$$

**Answer.** $1.59$.

questions.subject: Surface Mining
questions.stem: A haul ramp descends to a depth of 90 m at a ruling gradient of 9% as shown. Compute the horizontal length (m) of the ramp.

figure.caption: Descending ramp
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 240 150' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><polygon points='20,30 220,130 20,130' fill='#fde9c8' stroke='#92400e'/><line x1='20' y1='30' x2='20' y2='130' stroke='#475569'/><text x='26' y='80' font-size='8'>depth 90 m</text><text x='100' y='108' font-size='8' transform='rotate(27 100 108)'>grade 9%</text></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Gradient = rise ÷ horizontal run, so run = rise ÷ gradient.

**Working.**
$$L = \frac{90}{0.09} = 1000$$

**Answer.** $\mathbf{1000}$ m of horizontal length.

questions.subject: Underground Mining
questions.stem: A rock bolt of 22 mm diameter is made of steel with a yield strength of 400 MPa. Its yield load, in kN, is ____ (nearest whole number).
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Yield load $=$ yield stress $\times$ cross-sectional area.

**Working.**
$$A=\frac{\pi}{4}(22)^2=380.1\ \text{mm}^2$$
$$P=400\times380.1=152{,}040\ \text{N}\approx152\ \text{kN}$$

**Answer.** 152 kN.

questions.subject: Drilling & Blasting
questions.stem: Sympathetic detonation refers to
options: an explosive burning slowly instead of detonating
the detonation of one charge being initiated by the shock of a neighbouring charge across the intervening ground
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Shock can jump between nearby charges.

**Reasoning.** If holes are too close, the detonation of one can set off an adjacent charge through the ground (sympathetic detonation), upsetting the intended delay sequence and raising the charge per delay — hence minimum hole-spacing rules.

**Answer.** One charge initiating an adjacent charge via shock.

questions.subject: Rock Mechanics
questions.stem: A pillar has strength $80\,\text{MPa}$ and carries a stress of $40\,\text{MPa}$. The factor of safety is _____ .
questions.solution:
**Concept.** $\text{FoS}=\dfrac{\text{pillar strength}}{\text{pillar stress}}$.

**Working.**
$$\text{FoS}=\frac{80}{40}=2.$$

**Answer.** $2$.

questions.subject: Rock Mechanics
questions.stem: In the triaxial test shown, a cohesionless rock has $\phi=30^\circ$ and is subjected to a confining pressure $\sigma_3=10\ \mathrm{MPa}$. Using $\sigma_1=\sigma_3\tan^2(45^\circ+\phi/2)$, the major principal stress at failure, in MPa, is _____.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 180 190' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><rect x='70' y='50' width='40' height='90' fill='#d6d3d1' stroke='#475569'/><g stroke='#dc2626' stroke-width='2'><line x1='80' y1='25' x2='80' y2='48' marker-end='url(#t1)'/><line x1='100' y1='25' x2='100' y2='48' marker-end='url(#t1)'/></g><g stroke='#2563eb' stroke-width='2'><line x1='40' y1='95' x2='66' y2='95' marker-end='url(#t2)'/><line x1='140' y1='95' x2='114' y2='95' marker-end='url(#t2)'/></g><defs><marker id='t1' markerWidth='8' markerHeight='8' refX='4' refY='6' orient='auto'><path d='M0 0 L4 6 L8 0 Z' fill='#dc2626'/></marker><marker id='t2' markerWidth='8' markerHeight='8' refX='6' refY='3' orient='auto'><path d='M0 0 L6 3 L0 6 Z' fill='#2563eb'/></marker></defs><text x='104' y='35' font-size='9' fill='#dc2626'>s1</text><text x='6' y='90' font-size='9' fill='#2563eb'>s3=10</text></svg>
figure.caption: Triaxial test specimen

questions.solution:
**Concept.** $\sigma_1=\sigma_3\,N_\phi$ with $N_\phi=\tan^2(45^\circ+\phi/2)$.

**Working.**
$$N_\phi=\tan^2 60^\circ=3,\quad \sigma_1=10\times3=30\ \text{MPa}$$

**Answer.** $30$ MPa.

questions.subject: Mine Environment & Occupational Health
questions.stem: In India, the statutory body responsible for enforcing safety and health regulations in mines is the
options: Directorate General of Mines Safety (DGMS), under the Mines Act
questions.solution:
**Concept.** DGMS administers mine safety law.

**Reasoning.** The DGMS enforces the Mines Act and associated regulations (safety, health, gas/dust limits, inspections), distinct from environmental regulators such as the Pollution Control Boards.

**Answer.** DGMS.

questions.subject: mine-surveying
questions.stem: A traverse line of length $100$ m has a whole-circle bearing of $60^\circ$. Compute its latitude (m).

figure.caption: Latitude of a line
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 180 170' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><line x1='40' y1='140' x2='40' y2='30' stroke='#cbd5e1'/><line x1='40' y1='140' x2='150' y2='140' stroke='#cbd5e1'/><line x1='40' y1='140' x2='135' y2='85' stroke='#2563eb' stroke-width='2'/><line x1='40' y1='140' x2='40' y2='85' stroke='#dc2626' stroke-width='2'/><text x='90' y='105' font-size='8'>l=100</text><text x='52' y='124' font-size='8'>60 deg</text><text x='22' y='25' font-size='8'>N</text></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Latitude $L=l\cos\theta$.

**Working.**
$$L=100\cos60^\circ=100\times0.5=50\ \text{m}$$

**Answer.** $50$ m.

questions.subject: Mine Economics, Planning & Systems Engineering
questions.stem: On the time line shown, a sum of $\mathrm{Rs}\,121$ is received at the end of year 2. At a discount rate of $10\%$ per annum, its present value (at year 0), in Rs, is _____.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 240 120' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><line x1='20' y1='60' x2='220' y2='60' stroke='#475569'/><g fill='#1f2937'><circle cx='40' cy='60' r='3'/><circle cx='130' cy='60' r='3'/><circle cx='210' cy='60' r='3'/></g><text x='36' y='50' font-size='9'>0</text><text x='126' y='50' font-size='9'>1</text><text x='206' y='50' font-size='9'>2</text><text x='176' y='86' font-size='9' fill='#16a34a'>FV = 121</text><text x='100' y='102' font-size='9'>i = 10%</text></svg>
figure.caption: Discounting a future cash flow

questions.subject: Mine Environment & Occupational Health
questions.stem: A flame safety lamp flame is extinguished when the oxygen content of the air falls below about
questions.solution:
**Concept.** A flame needs sufficient oxygen.

**Reasoning.** Below roughly 16% O₂ the lamp flame can no longer be sustained and goes out — a warning of dangerous oxygen deficiency, even though such air can still be briefly breathed.

**Answer.** About 16%.

questions.subject: Surface Mining
questions.stem: For the pit wall shown with vertical height 60 m and horizontal projection 120 m, compute the overall slope angle (degrees).

figure.caption: Overall pit slope
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 240 150' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><polygon points='20,30 200,130 20,130' fill='#fde9c8' stroke='#92400e'/><line x1='20' y1='30' x2='20' y2='130' stroke='#475569'/><text x='10' y='84' font-size='8' transform='rotate(-90 12 84)'>H=60</text><text x='92' y='145' font-size='8'>L=120</text></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** The overall slope angle is $\theta = \tan^{-1}(H/L)$.

**Working.**
$$\theta = \tan^{-1}\!\left(\frac{60}{120}\right) = \tan^{-1}(0.5) = 26.57^\circ$$

**Answer.** $\approx \mathbf{26.57^\circ}$.
